scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: Multilingual classrooms. [pause] When a student doesn't speak the language of instruction — every lesson is an accessibility challenge.

scenes.name: The reach
scenes.text: Language barriers cut across every subject. [pause] Maths problem statements. Science instructions. History passages. Even PE — verbal instructions that leave the student standing still while classmates begin.

scenes.name: The shift
scenes.text: Before AI, a student waited. [pause] After AI, the worksheet, the instruction, the reading — translated in seconds, into their mother tongue. [pause] Subject content becomes immediately accessible.

scenes.name: Tools
scenes.text: AI can do more than translate. [pause] Direct translation. Language-level adaptation — explain at B1, use everyday words. [pause] Or selective help — define just the words they don't know.

scenes.name: Voice
scenes.text: Voice mode is powerful here. [pause] The student speaks in Ukrainian. The AI explains in simplified Croatian. [pause] The instruction lands. And the target language is heard in a context the student already understands.

scenes.name: Vocab
scenes.text: Technical vocabulary needs special handling. [pause] Translate the text, but keep the scientific terms in the language of instruction. [pause] The student understands — and learns the term they will meet on the test.

scenes.name: Cultural
scenes.text: Translation is linguistic — not cultural. [pause] An example about splitting a restaurant bill, a reference to local history — these may need human context. [pause] AI gives equivalence. You give meaning.

scenes.name: Asset
scenes.text: And reframe the perspective. [pause] Multilingual students bring cognitive and cultural assets. [pause] Use AI to translate — but also to celebrate. Show us how this concept is expressed in your language.
